sources: United Nations Economic Commission for Africa

items.headline: US and Iran exchange fire in Strait of Hormuz
items.body: American and Iranian forces exchanged missile and drone fire in the Strait of Hormuz. The clash tests a fragile ceasefire, raising risks for global oil shipping.

items.headline: African nations forge joint strategy on debt and climate finance
items.body: A sovereign finance forum concluded in Ethiopia on Friday. The meeting aimed to unify Africa's approach to debt relief and climate funding.

items.headline: Asian Development Bank launches $70 billion infrastructure plan
items.body: The bank announced a major energy and digital connectivity initiative for Asia. It aims to provide power and internet to hundreds of millions of people.

es.headline: Government orders virus-hit ship to dock over regional objections
es.body: The central government ordered a cruise ship with hantavirus cases to dock in Tenerife. This move overrides the Canary Islands government, sparking a conflict over regional health autonomy.

no.headline: Norway commits to reopening three offshore gas fields
no.body: The government announced it will restart three gas fields by 2028. This decision aims to secure long-term energy supplies to Europe.

uk.headline: UK grants license for major new gas storage facility
uk.body: The project is expected to double Britain's current gas storage capacity. This move seeks to enhance national energy security and reduce reliance on imports.

nl.headline: Central bank finds capital and labor trapped in unproductive firms
nl.body: The Dutch central bank reported capital and labor are stuck in unproductive firms. This pressures the government to pursue reforms, shifting jobs and business investment patterns.
